steps.title: Fit the shown line first
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)-11$. Put two points from the graph into a Desmos table: $(-2,0)$ and $(0,-4)$. The regression line has slope $-2$ and intercept $-4$, so the graphed line is $y=-2x-4$.

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content: Since the graph is $y=f(x)-11$, add 11 to the fitted equation: $$f(x)=(-2x-4)+11=-2x+7$$

steps.title: Check choices in Desmos
steps.content: Choice B gives $f(x)=-2x+7$. Subtracting 11 gives $y=-2x-4$, exactly the graphed line. Choice C has the same intercept after shifting, but its slope is $-13$, not $-2$. Choices A and D miss the intercept.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: $f(x)=-2x+7$ matches choice B. Choice B is correct.
